summary: The diaphysis is the tubular shaft of a long bone, while the epiphysis is the expanded end.
The diaphysis is the expanded end of a long bone, while the epiphysis is the shaft.
The metaphysis is the tubular shaft, while the diaphysis is the growth zone.
The metaphysis is the transitional zone between diaphysis and epiphysis containing the growth plate.
The metaphysis is the central marrow cavity within the diaphysis.
The epiphyseal plate is located within the diaphysis, not the metaphysis.
Nutrient arteries point towards the elbow in upper limb bones and away from the knee in lower limb bones.
Nutrient arteries always point towards the nearest joint in all long bones.
Nutrient arteries point away from the elbow and towards the knee.
The clavicle is the only long bone that ossifies in membrane and has no medullary cavity.
The fibula is the only long bone that ossifies in membrane.
The clavicle ossifies in cartilage like all other long bones.
Hilton's law states that the nerve supplying a joint also supplies the muscles moving it and the overlying skin.
Hilton's law states that muscles are always supplied by the nerve closest to their origin.
Hilton's law describes how blood supply to a bone determines its growth.
The periosteum covers the outer surface of bone and is responsible for appositional growth.
The endosteum covers the outer surface of bone and repairs fractures.
The periosteum lines the medullary cavity and produces red blood cells.
